scope: Faithful KalmanNet SPOT-OD transposition under a separately predeclared rule (loop57). The transposition addresses, jointly and as predeclared adaptations, the four design changes enumerated in the prior SPOT-OD design-gap statement: (i) orbital-scale state and observation normalization through fixed scale vectors applied uniformly to the state target, the noisy initial state, and the per-station observation vector before the learned gain network sees them; (ii) sequence-length and curriculum rematching through end-to-end recurrent training over the full 120-step SPOT-OD arc on the observed-step evaluation window so the gain network sees the same temporal context every classical baseline sees; (iii) sparse-observation architectural adaptation through identical zeroing of invisible station blocks in both the measurement vector and the predicted observation, with the per-step visibility mask propagated through the recurrent forward pass; (iv) learning-rate and budget recalibration to a smaller-batch lower-rate regime appropriate for the larger m=6, n=32 system relative to the upstream linear-canonical (m=2, n=2) benchmark. The trained KalmanGain RNN is taken unmodified from the official upstream release at the recorded commit; only the system dimensions, sequence length, and the f, h bridges are adapted, and these adaptations are documented in the result artifact.
rationale: The loop56 paper review identified the SPOT-OD design-gap statement as the load-bearing rhetorical gap in the loop56 manuscript: the audit's claim that learned-versus-classical OD comparisons attrite under the harness is partly defended by a faithful KalmanNet upstream-architecture sanity reproduction on the linear-canonical benchmark plus a design-gap statement, rather than by a faithful transposition outcome on SPOT-OD itself. This rule converts the design-gap statement into an evidential transposition outcome under a separately predeclared positive criterion: regardless of the sign of the outcome the audit position becomes evidential rather than rhetorical. The four design changes enumerated above are recorded jointly as the predeclared adaptations, so the comparison is between the upstream architecture under documented orbital-scale, sequence-length, sparse-observation, and budget adaptations and the manuscript's classical references on the same held-out test population.

vendor.modifications_to_upstream: None to the gain network. The f, h bridges, the step-counter wrapper, and the visibility-mask propagation are SPOT-OD-side glue code. The trainer in the loop57 driver is a custom held-out-validation-best loop that is structurally identical to the upstream Pipeline_EKF training contract (MSE state loss, held-out validation-best model selection).

predeclared_design_changes.orbital_scale_normalization: Position states are divided by 1e7 m and velocity states by 1e4 m/s before being passed to the gain network; range observations are divided by 1e7 m, azimuth and elevation by their respective angular scales, and range-rate by 1e4 m/s. The same scaling is applied to the noisy initial state estimate so the gain network sees unit-scale signals as in the upstream linear-canonical benchmark.
predeclared_design_changes.sequence_length_and_curriculum_rematchin: Training and held-out validation use the full SPOT-OD 120-step arc at dt=20 s; the gain network is trained end-to-end through the full recurrent sequence with MSE on the observed-step evaluation window (steps 11 to 119 inclusive), matching the manuscript classical baselines' scoring window. No curriculum reweighting beyond the observed-step evaluation window is applied; the same window is used for all classical baselines.
predeclared_design_changes.sparse_observation_architectural_adaptat: For an invisible station block at the current step, both the measurement-vector entry and the network's predicted-measurement entry are set to zero identically. The innovation on invisible channels is therefore exactly zero and the gain network sees no information to exploit on those channels. The per-step visibility mask is propagated through the forward pass via a system-functions wrapper.
predeclared_design_changes.learning_rate_and_budget_recalibration: Predeclared learning rate 1.0e-4, predeclared weight decay 1.0e-4, predeclared gradient-norm clip 1.0, predeclared upstream architecture multipliers in_mult_KNet=5 and out_mult_KNet=4 (the latter reduced from the upstream linear-canonical default of 40 to keep parameter count bounded for the larger m=6, n=32 system; this is a documented predeclared adaptation, not a tuning sweep).

data.scenario: SPOT-OD compact two-body+J2+exponential-density-drag dynamics, eight-station ground network, range/azimuth/elevation/range-rate per visible station, identical to the manuscript main nominal scenario.

train_split.purpose: End-to-end training of the gain RNN.

validation_split.purpose: Held-out validation for model selection (validation-best step selection). Disjoint from train and test.

test_split.purpose: Held-out test for the predeclared decision predicate. Disjoint from train, validation, and every model-selection seed used in the manuscript.

predeclared_optimisation_budget.model_selection_rule: Validation-best step (lowest CV MSE on the held-out validation split) selected during training and re-loaded before test evaluation. No test-set information enters the selection. The budget is a predeclared adaptation of the loop42 60-step and loop45 1000-step training schedules: 300 steps balances training depth against the computational budget available for one loop, and it is committed in this artefact before the held-out test seed is evaluated.

primary_endpoint: Mean observed-step position RMSE on the held-out test split (matches the manuscript primary endpoint used by every classical baseline).
decision_predicate: The KalmanNet SPOT-OD transposition is a positive contribution if and only if (i) its mean observed-step position RMSE on the held-out test split is strictly the lowest among {KalmanNet-SPOT-OD, EKF, UKF, AUKF, PUKF}, AND (ii) the paired-bootstrap 95% CI for the KalmanNet-SPOT-OD minus best-non-KalmanNet gap is strictly negative, AND (iii) the absolute gap exceeds the predeclared 3% practical-significance floor of the best-non-KalmanNet observed-step position RMSE.
honest_negative: If the predeclared rule does not yield a positive in the sense above, the transposition is reported as an external faithful learned-OD bounded negative under the predeclared design changes, with the four design changes recorded jointly as the predeclared adaptations. No rule retuning is allowed after the held-out test evaluation. The result is reported regardless of the sign of the outcome; this artefact governs the loop57 SPOT-OD KalmanNet headline regardless of whether the criterion is met.
